seoTitle: Record COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record Cost of Goods Sold in QuickBooks Online to get an accurate view of profitability for products, materials, and inventory-based sales.
intro: Understanding your true profitability starts with tracking what it actually costs to produce or acquire what you sell. This guide covers how to record Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) in QuickBooks Online, whether you're a retailer, manufacturer, or service provider with material costs. It's aimed at small business owners and accountants who want clearer visibility into margins.

steps.heading: Set up a COGS account
steps.body: Create a dedicated Cost of Goods Sold account in your chart of accounts if one doesn't already exist.

steps.heading: Link products to COGS
steps.body: When setting up inventory items, associate each product with the COGS account so costs are tracked automatically as items sell.

steps.heading: Record costs at the point of sale
steps.body: As sales are recorded, QuickBooks will move the associated cost into the COGS account, reflecting the expense against the matching revenue.

steps.heading: Review profitability reports
steps.body: Use QuickBooks' profit and loss report to see how COGS is affecting your gross margin across different products or services.

steps.heading: Adjust for accuracy
steps.body: Periodically review inventory costs and adjust entries if actual material or production costs change.

faq.question: Do service-based businesses need to track COGS?
faq.answer: Yes, if a service involves direct costs like materials or subcontractor labor, tracking those as COGS gives a more accurate profitability picture.

faq.question: How does COGS differ from regular business expenses?
faq.answer: COGS reflects costs directly tied to producing what you sell, while regular expenses cover overhead like rent or marketing.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks automatically calculate COGS for inventory items?
faq.answer: Yes, if inventory tracking is set up correctly, QuickBooks calculates COGS automatically as items are sold.

faq.question: Why is tracking COGS important for small businesses?
faq.answer: It reveals your true profit margins, helping you make informed pricing and inventory decisions.
